title: goodyera ovatilabia
intro: Goodyera ovatilabia, also known as the ovate-labia rattlesnake plantain, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating snakebites, and alleviating skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Goodyera ovatilabia are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
